epilogue_zero_burn.text: Three days passed. Or maybe it was longer. Time moved differently when you didn't have a name to mark it with.
The man who had been Arrel sat at the edge of The Seam, watching colors he couldn't name bleed across the canyon walls.
BL-07 was sealed. The ground was solid. Children played in the gardens where flowers bloomed in impossible seasons.

epilogue_zero_burn.speaker: Elia
epilogue_zero_burn.text: You've been sitting here since dawn.
Have I? I can't tell.

epilogue_zero_burn.speaker: Elia
epilogue_zero_burn.text: Do you... want me to tell you? About who you were?
He looked at her. The woman with silver hair. He knew she was important. He just didn't know why.
Would it change anything?

epilogue_zero_burn.speaker: Elia
epilogue_zero_burn.text: It might change everything.
She sat down beside him. Close enough that their shoulders almost touched.

epilogue_zero_burn.speaker: Elia
epilogue_zero_burn.text: Your name was Arrel. You were someone who burned pieces of himself to keep other people warm.

epilogue_zero_burn.speaker: Elia
epilogue_zero_burn.text: You were kind. Stubborn. Terrible at cooking.
Something stirred. Not a memory. Memories were gone. But a feeling — like the shape of a word you've forgotten, still pressing against the inside of your mouth.
...Arrel.

epilogue_zero_burn.speaker: Elia
epilogue_zero_burn.text: Yes.
He said it again. Not remembering. Just... trying it on.

epilogue_zero_burn.speaker: Arrel
epilogue_zero_burn.text: Arrel.
The name didn't fit. Not anymore. But it was a start.

epilogue_zero_burn.speaker: Sable
epilogue_zero_burn.text: The Seam will hold. Thanks to what he did.

epilogue_zero_burn.speaker: Sable
epilogue_zero_burn.text: There are other Void Holes. Other places that need someone who can burn.

epilogue_zero_burn.speaker: Elia
epilogue_zero_burn.text: He's not burning anything else.

epilogue_zero_burn.speaker: Sable
epilogue_zero_burn.text: That's his choice. It always was.
The sun set over The Seam. The colors deepened — amber, crimson, that impossible green.
Somewhere, in the space where a name used to be, something new was beginning to grow.
Not a memory. Something better.
A choice to stay.
— MEMORIA: The Price of Oblivion —
ZERO BURN ENDING: The name is gone. But the man remains.

epilogue_preservation.text: They returned to The Seam with the Void Hole still open behind them.
Sable was waiting at the entrance. Her face said she already knew.

epilogue_preservation.speaker: Sable
epilogue_preservation.text: It's still growing.

epilogue_preservation.speaker: Arrel
epilogue_preservation.text: I know.

epilogue_preservation.speaker: Sable
epilogue_preservation.text: You could have ended it.

epilogue_preservation.speaker: Arrel
epilogue_preservation.text: By ending myself? That's not a solution. That's a transaction.

epilogue_preservation.speaker: Sable
epilogue_preservation.text: Everything is a transaction.

epilogue_preservation.speaker: Arrel
epilogue_preservation.text: Not this.
Silence. Then Sable did something unexpected. She smiled.

epilogue_preservation.speaker: Sable
epilogue_preservation.text: The last team I sent in — they burned everything they had. Grade 5 down to Grade 1. And the hole still opened again six months later.

epilogue_preservation.speaker: Sable
epilogue_preservation.text: Maybe burning isn't the answer. Maybe it never was.

epilogue_preservation.speaker: Elia
epilogue_preservation.text: Then what is?

epilogue_preservation.speaker: Sable
epilogue_preservation.text: I don't know. But I've been collecting data. Every Void Hole, every burn, every seal that held and every one that didn't.

epilogue_preservation.speaker: Sable
epilogue_preservation.text: There's a pattern. I just can't see it yet.

epilogue_preservation.speaker: Arrel
epilogue_preservation.text: Then we find it.
He looked at his hands. The hands that could burn. That had burned. That still carried the heat of every memory he'd ever sacrificed.
But also hands that could hold. That could build. That could choose NOT to destroy.

epilogue_preservation.speaker: Arrel
epilogue_preservation.text: My name is Arrel. I remember who gave it to me. And I'm keeping it.
Behind them, BL-07 pulsed. Patient. Hungry.
But The Seam was still here. And so was he. All of him.
For now, that was enough.
— MEMORIA: The Price of Oblivion —
PRESERVATION ENDING: The hole remains. So does the name. The search continues.

elia_epilogue_talk.speaker: Elia
elia_epilogue_talk.text: I've been thinking about what Sable said. About the pattern.

elia_epilogue_talk.speaker: Elia
elia_epilogue_talk.text: The Void Holes appear where too many memories have been burned. Not just individual memories — collective ones. Shared histories.

elia_epilogue_talk.speaker: Elia
elia_epilogue_talk.text: What if the world isn't forgetting on its own? What if we're MAKING it forget?
The implication hung in the air like ash.

elia_epilogue_talk.speaker: Arrel
elia_epilogue_talk.text: Then every burn I've ever done—

elia_epilogue_talk.speaker: Elia
elia_epilogue_talk.text: But you didn't know. None of us did.
She took his hand. It was the first time she'd done that.

sable_epilogue_talk.speaker: Sable
sable_epilogue_talk.text: Most burners don't stay. The ones who survive... they keep moving. Keep burning. Until there's nothing left.
She paused, looking out over The Seam's impossible gardens.

sable_epilogue_talk.speaker: Sable
sable_epilogue_talk.text: There's a settlement three days east. They're losing memories faster than normal. Not burning — just... losing them.
